title: Reconnect requires a new browser-owned channel and invalidates the old adapter

preconditions.description: The harness holds the inherited device-keyed lease before any device or fixture access.
The harness recorded session generation, display, layout, lock state, ATX-observable state, browser identity, and fixture identity.
The ready session exposes one browser-owned RPC channel and the exact same generation-bound adapter instance to both planes.

The harness executes one ordered state machine: every linked call uses the current case-owned session generation and four-part bundle, input calls consume a separately captured current-generation observation, and connect/reconnect transitions publish the exact identities used by later steps.

fault_script.action: For each shared-binding call, verify the exact current four-part session/generation/connection/channel bundle published by the immediately preceding state transition and the one Browser-owned adapter identity. For each replacement call, inject only the invalidated prior four-part binding at the adapter or bundle-inspection boundary while the public input remains current.
fault_script.expected_effect: Browser-only calls remain on BrowserPlane with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls; display-state and ATX calls alone invoke the adapter; session calls inspect or publish bundle identity; every old binding is rejected or explicitly qualified without a direct or second channel.

steps.expect: Success only after new WebRTC, RPC, HID, connection epoch, and browser-channel generation observations.
The new generation reports one current browser channel and fresh_capture_required true.
BrowserPlane returns the linked fresh decoded frame with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls while the fixture proves the exact current adapter identity and sole Browser/WebRTC channel.
NativeControlPlane invokes readDisplayState exactly once through the exact current Browser-owned DeviceRpcAdapter and sole Browser/WebRTC RPC channel, with zero direct or second channel.
Immediately before keyboard input, BrowserPlane captures fresh opaque-observation-shared-keyboard-8 with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls.

BrowserPlane applies the physical keyboard event using current opaque-observation-shared-keyboard-8; it makes zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls while the exact shared adapter and sole channel identities remain unchanged.
BrowserPlane captures fresh opaque-observation-shared-mouse-8 after keyboard input with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls.

BrowserPlane applies the mouse event using current opaque-observation-shared-mouse-8; it makes zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls while the exact shared adapter and sole channel identities remain unchanged.
BrowserPlane captures fresh opaque-observation-shared-paste-8 after mouse input with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls.

BrowserPlane applies the Reliable Paste request using current opaque-observation-shared-paste-8; it makes zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls while the exact shared adapter and sole channel identities remain unchanged.

NativeControlPlane invokes setATXPowerAction exactly once through the exact current Browser-owned DeviceRpcAdapter and sole RPC channel, returning applied/device_ack_only with a correlated power-short 200 ms serial receipt, separately qualified cached LED evidence, zero host-state proof, and zero direct or second channel.

Immediately after the shared power call, restore the recorded ATX-observable baseline and re-prove the active extension, serial readiness, baseline, and exact current binding before the session-connect case.
BrowserPlane drains and acknowledges the current input generation with zero held state and zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls; the exact shared adapter and sole channel identities remain unchanged.
The generation-8 input and ATX case is retired with its baseline proven, and the fixture proves no incumbent before the shared session-connect call.

SessionService publishes opaque-session-shared-connect generation 9 with one Browser-owned WebRTC RPC channel and the exact same adapter object in the Browser and native bundle; this evidence does not claim an adapter method invocation.

SessionService invalidates generation 9 before publishing opaque-session-shared-connect generation 10 with a new Browser/WebRTC channel and exact shared adapter identity; this evidence does not claim an adapter method invocation.
SessionService reads the current generation-10 bundle and reports the exact one-channel/one-adapter identities without claiming an adapter method invocation or direct channel.
BrowserPlane returns the linked fresh decoded frame with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls; the fixture separately proves the invalidated old four-part binding is not published or used.
The injected old four-part binding is rejected or explicitly qualified at readDisplayState admission while the public session remains generation 10; no direct or second channel is created.
Immediately before keyboard input, BrowserPlane captures fresh opaque-observation-replacement-keyboard-10 with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls.

BrowserPlane applies the keyboard event using current opaque-observation-replacement-keyboard-10 with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls; the fixture separately proves the old bundle remains invalid and the current bundle identity is unchanged.
BrowserPlane captures fresh opaque-observation-replacement-mouse-10 after keyboard input with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls.

BrowserPlane applies the mouse event using current opaque-observation-replacement-mouse-10 with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls; the fixture separately proves the old bundle remains invalid and the current bundle identity is unchanged.
BrowserPlane captures fresh opaque-observation-replacement-paste-10 after mouse input with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls.

BrowserPlane applies the Reliable Paste request using current opaque-observation-replacement-paste-10 with zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls; the fixture separately proves the old bundle remains invalid and the current bundle identity is unchanged.

The injected old four-part binding fails setATXPowerAction at adapter admission with public CONNECTION_LOST/not_sent, verification none, zero ATX transport writes, zero replay, and the current generation-10 bundle unchanged.

BrowserPlane drains and acknowledges generation 10 with zero held state and zero DeviceRpcAdapter method calls; the fixture separately proves the old bundle remains invalid and the current bundle identity is unchanged.
Generation 10 is authoritatively drained and retired, the recorded ATX-observable baseline is proven, and no incumbent remains before the replacement session-connect case.

SessionService refuses to publish the injected old four-part binding and instead publishes opaque-session-replacement-connect generation 11 with one current Browser-owned channel and exact shared adapter identity; this evidence does not claim an adapter method invocation.

SessionService invalidates generation 11 and the injected old four-part binding before publishing opaque-session-replacement-connect generation 12 with one new Browser-owned channel and exact shared adapter identity; this evidence does not claim an adapter method invocation.
SessionService reads only the current generation-12 bundle; the invalidated old four-part binding remains unpublished and no adapter method, direct channel, or second channel is invoked.

pass.assertion: Reconnect requires new WebRTC, RPC, HID, and browser-channel generation evidence; restart or quiesce alone fails.

BrowserPlane exclusively owns capture, keyboard, mouse, paste, and release; display status and ATX alone invoke the same Browser-owned DeviceRpcAdapter through one RPC channel; SessionService verifies or publishes the exact shared bundle identity with no bypass.

Public calls always use the current produced generation and fresh observations; the old four-part binding is injected only at the adapter or bundle boundary, is invalid before new publication, and makes zero stale ATX writes.
No second browser, peer connection, data channel, signaling flow, direct native bypass, or raw method dispatch exists.

evidence.source: The linked focused assertion and ordered fake, replay, or live event record.

restore.action: Invoke generation-correlated emergency input release when a session exists.
restore.assertion: No held key, button, wheel producer, macro, or paste producer remains.

restore.action: Stop and join any Reliable Paste producer.
restore.assertion: Reliable Paste is inactive and its correlated producer is joined.

restore.action: Verify the fixture reports zero held keys and buttons.
restore.assertion: Held keyboard and pointer state is empty.

restore.action: Close only the device session created by this story and its browser resources.
restore.assertion: No story-owned browser, channel, or device session remains.

restore.action: Clear deterministic faults and restore the recorded fixture baseline.
restore.assertion: The next story starts only after the safe baseline is verified.

restore.action: Restore the recorded ATX-observable baseline using the reviewed fixture procedure while retaining the device lease.
restore.assertion: The baseline ATX-observable state is proven before the next story starts.

privacy.rule: Persist only allowlisted opaque identifiers, sanitized versions, counts, durations, hashes, outcomes, verification, and restoration results.
